string.text: Seamless Inventory Sort
General
Looting and Bodies
Loot Highlight
Performance

Keeps a SortingPlus inventory tidy: when an item lands out of order (gun modified, item moved, traded, marked favorite/junk) it snaps back into its sorted position in place, with no flicker and without losing your scroll position.
Enable
Master switch. When off, inventories behave like vanilla SortingPlus (items land at the end until you reopen the window).
Seamless Sort in inventory window
Keep your own inventory sorted as you modify, move or pick up items. Turn off to let items land at the end in the inventory screen (and the repair screen) until you reopen it.
Seamless Sort in loot / stash window

Keep both sides sorted while looting bodies, stashes and containers. Turn off to leave the loot screen unsorted as you take and drop items. (The body and Looting Takes Time options on the next page still apply.)
Seamless Sort in trade window
Keep both sale bags sorted while trading. Turn off to leave the trade screen unsorted as you move items in and out of the basket.
Re-sort on favorite / junk
When you mark an item favorite or junk (SortingPlus K / J keys or the right-click menu), snap it to the top / bottom instantly in place. When off, SortingPlus' own behavior is used.
Keep gaps after removing items

When you drop, sell or stash an item and nothing else moved, leave the empty slot where it was instead of repacking everything below it. The gap fills back in next time something is added. Turn off to always repack immediately on removal.
Keep modified weapons in the bag

When you attach or detach a scope, silencer or grenade launcher on a weapon in your bag, the engine rebuilds the weapon and equips it to an empty weapon slot, so it jumps out of your bag into the primary / secondary slot. Turn on to move it straight back to the bag so it stays where it was. Turn off to let the engine equip it.
How looted dead bodies are handled. Only dead bodies are affected on this page; stashes, containers and your own inventory always sort.
Looted body handling

What to do with a looted dead body's items. Only dead bodies are affected; stashes, containers, companions and your own bag always sort.

- Sort normally: sort the body like any container.
- Never sort - keep as found: leave the body unsorted (size-kind layout), the same as SortingPlus' "sort npc inventories" off, but for corpses only. No Looting Takes Time needed.
- Keep Looting Takes Time order: never sort; leave the body exactly in the order Looting Takes Time revealed it. (Looting Takes Time only; behaves like "Sort normally" without it.)
- Sort after Looting Takes Time reveal: wait for the reveal to finish, then sort once and keep it sorted as you loot. (Looting Takes Time only.)
Sort normally
Never sort - keep as found
Keep Looting Takes Time order
Sort after Looting Takes Time reveal
Tint items you just took from a container, stash or body with a colored background, so you can see at a glance what you found. The highlight clears when you close the inventory.
Highlight new loot
Enable the new-loot highlight. Use the sliders below to choose the color.
Color - Red
Red component of the highlight color (0-255).
Color - Green
Green component of the highlight color (0-255).
Color - Blue
Blue component of the highlight color (0-255).
Intensity
Opacity of the highlight (0-255). Low values give a subtle tint; high values a strong, solid color. Default 60.

Anti-oscillation guard. Some items have an unstable sort key (e.g. a magazine while it is being bound) that can make a container re-sort every refresh and visibly churn. This detects that and briefly stops re-sorting so the list settles.
Anti-oscillation guard
Back off from re-sorting a container that is churning. Leave on unless troubleshooting.
Re-sorts before back-off
How many re-sorts of one container within a one-second window are allowed before the guard decides it is oscillating. Lower = more aggressive. Default 8.
Back-off time (sec)
How long the guard stops re-sorting an oscillating container so it can settle, then seamless sorting resumes. Default 1.5 s.
